title: The ‘Make America Healthy Again’ Movement Is Cooling on Trump and Republicans
summary: Vaccine skeptics, ‘organic moms’ and environmental activists came together to elect President Trump. But some voters are disillusioned and might not turn out again.

title: The Generals Who Are Now Running Iran
summary: The killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei ushered in a new form of collective leadership in the country, with more power for the Revolutionary Guards.

title: How People in Lebanon Are Reacting to Talks With Israel
summary: The Lebanese are deeply divided over whether rare direct talks with Israel are a good thing.

title: House Republicans Ask ActBlue C.E.O., Regina Wallace-Jones, to Testify
summary: Hours after Republican lawmakers asked the Democratic fund-raising group’s chief executive to testify, ActBlue sent a sharp letter dismissing the inquiry as a “partisan attack.”

title: Candidates for California Governor Debate: 5 Takeaways
summary: The nonpartisan primary remains volatile after the departure of Eric Swalwell. A televised debate featured six leading candidates, but produced few fireworks.

title: An Independent Senate Hopeful Tries to Scare Off Montana Democrats
summary: Seth Bodnar, an independent, could have the best chance of breaking the Republican stranglehold in Montana, but the state’s Democrats are choosing to fight.

title: Meta to Lay Off 10 Percent of Work Force in A.I. Push
summary: The layoffs affect about 8,000 employees, with Meta also planning to close 6,000 open roles, as the company focuses on artificial intelligence.

title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s Epic Fight Heads to Court
summary: A jury trial that is set to start on Monday could shift the course of the A.I. race, with Mr. Musk seeking billions of dollars in damages from Mr. Altman’s OpenAI.

title: OpenAI Unveils Its New, More Powerful GPT-5.5 Model
summary: The maker of ChatGPT is taking a more open approach to cybersecurity than its chief rival, Anthropic.

title: After Cesar Chavez’s Fall, What to Do With the Art That Honored Him?
summary: Artists who created public depictions of the civil rights icon Cesar Chavez have had to revisit their works after accusations emerged of Mr. Chavez’s sexual abuse of girls in the movement.

title: $106 Billion Loan Reflects E.U.’s View That Peace in Ukraine Is Far Away
summary: Unlike previous European assistance packages, this one is heavily weighted toward military spending, meant to put Ukraine on solid footing for a long fight.

title: Will Bulgaria’s New Leader Cast His Lot With Europe or Russia?
summary: With a record of pro-Russia statements, Rumen Radev handily won elections this week. Now Bulgarians and his E.U. partners wait to see which way he will turn.

title: Russian Officials Say Town’s Air Is Toxic, Days After Strike on Oil Refinery
summary: Since Ukraine attacked the refinery in Tuapse, starting a huge fire, residents have reported drops of dark, oily toxins falling from the sky.

title: Michael Tilson Thomas, Celebrated American Conductor, Dies at 81
summary: A galvanizing force in classical music as a conductor, composer, pianist and evangelist, he spent 25 years as music director of the San Francisco Symphony.

title: How the Brothers Behind Manhattan’s Aicon Art Gallery Found Themselves in Infinite Feuds
summary: For a decade and a half, Prajit and Projjal Dutta have led the market for South Asian art in the United States. Now they rarely speak to each other, except through lawyers.

title: He Was Exonerated in a Murder and Elected to Office. He May Never Serve.
summary: Calvin Duncan, who became a lawyer and an advocate for incarcerated people, was recently elected criminal court clerk in New Orleans. Lawmakers are racing to eliminate the role.

title: New Gene Therapy Enables Children With a Rare Form of Deafness to Hear
summary: The treatment, the first of its kind, was approved by the Food and Drug Administration on Thursday. “Our baby was born deaf, and now he can hear,” said one parent.

title: China Publishes Maps Detailing Minerals on the Ocean Floor
summary: The new deep-sea atlas underscores Beijing’s interest in ocean mining, its military ambitions and its claims to disputed waters.

title: A New Bureau Will Oversee Both Offshore Drilling and Seabed Mining
summary: The new federal office will undo a change made after the 2010 Deepwater Horizon oil disaster. Critics say it could reduce environmental oversight.

title: Trump Wants Private Equity and Crypto Accessible in 401(k)s. There Are Risks.
summary: Alternative investments could appear on some 401(k) menus in the year ahead. Here’s what that means for investors.

title: Anne Hathaway, Queen of Effort, Is Finally Ready to Vibe
summary: Known best for tightly wound characters in generational hit films, the actress is a tortured pop star in “Mother Mary” and returns to playful form in “The Devil Wears Prada 2.”

title: And Then There Were None, Trump Administration Edition
summary: Normal language is inadequate and precedents are irrelevant when it comes to appraising the senior administration officials of the president’s second term.

title: ‘People Here Do Not Consider Themselves Poor. They Consider Themselves Broke.’
summary: Big parts of the Rio Grande Valley turned against the party in recent years. One candidate is trying to win it back, one quinceañera at a time.

title: Some Labubu Dolls Contain Cotton Banned By Forced Labor Law, Testing Shows
summary: A test verified by The New York Times found that clothes for some of the viral dolls contained cotton from the Xinjiang region of China, which is banned in the United States.

title: Rodrigo Duterte Will Stand Trial for Crimes Against Humanity, Court Rules
summary: The International Criminal Court found there was enough evidence to try Rodrigo Duterte, the former Philippine president, for killings carried out during his crackdown on drugs.

title: Mamdani Faces Pressure to Cancel $2 Billion Deal to Expand Hudson Yards
summary: Related Companies struck a deal with Eric Adams to have New York City finance a costly platform to facilitate new housing. Mayor Zohran Mamdani said advancing the deal was not a priority.

title: Warner Bros. Discovery Shareholders Approve Paramount Deal
summary: The vote brings the tech scion David Ellison one step closer to uniting some of the world’s biggest news and entertainment properties.
